title: charred, smoky russian lotus-root pilaf
description: show-stopping side dish featuring caramelized renkon, ideal for special occasions or as a centerpiece for weeknight meals.
instructions: Cut the renkon into 1-inch pieces and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle with 2 tablespoons of olive oil and toss to coat. Roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until caramelized and tender.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and caramelized, about 20-25 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
If using wine, add it to the saucepan and cook until reduced by half, stirring occasionally.
Add the vegetable broth, dried thyme, and paprika. Stir to combine.
Add the roasted renkon to the saucepan and stir to coat with the liquid.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
To add an extra layer of flavor, caramelize the onion in a skillet over low heat for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally.
